mainEntity.name: How much does a new roof cost in Seattle?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most roof replacements in the Seattle area run between $12,000 and $35,000 depending on the size of your home, the pitch of the roof, and which shingle line you pick. A 2,000 square foot home with a standard slope typically falls in the $15,000 to $22,000 range with GAF HDZ shingles. We give free estimates with a full breakdown so you see exactly where the money goes. PNW homes often need extra attention to decking and ventilation because of the moisture we deal with year-round, and we include that in the quote upfront.

mainEntity.name: How long does a roof replacement take?

acceptedAnswer.text: We finish most residential roof replacements in 1 to 3 days. The timeline depends on the size of the roof, how many layers we need to tear off, and whether we find rot or damage in the decking underneath. We plan around weather because Seattle gives us plenty of rain days, and we won't install when conditions compromise the seal. You'll know the schedule before we start, and we stick to it.

mainEntity.name: Is Stark Roofing GAF Master Elite certified?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Stark Roofing & Renovation is a GAF Master Elite certified contractor, which puts us in the top 2% of roofing companies in the country. That certification means we can offer GAF's strongest warranty packages, including their Golden Pledge limited warranty. We maintain the certification every year through ongoing training and quality standards. It's not a badge we bought, it's something we earn by doing the work right.

mainEntity.name: Do you offer financing for roofing projects?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, we offer financing options so you can get your roof handled now and pay over time. A leaking roof in the Pacific Northwest only gets worse with each rain season, so waiting usually costs more in the long run. We'll walk you through what's available during your estimate appointment. Call us at (206) 739-8232 and we can go over the details for your specific project.

mainEntity.name: What areas near Bellevue does Stark Roofing serve?

acceptedAnswer.text: We serve Bellevue and the entire Eastside, including Redmond, Sammamish, Woodinville, Issaquah, Kirkland, and Renton. We also cover Seattle proper. Our crews work across all of these cities every week, so we're familiar with local permitting requirements and the specific roofing challenges each area deals with, from heavy tree cover in Sammamish to the older housing stock in Renton.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if I need a repair or full roof replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: If the damage is limited to a small section, like a few missing shingles or a single flashing leak, a repair usually makes sense. But if your roof is over 20 years old, has widespread moss damage, or you're seeing granule loss across most of the surface, replacement is the better investment. PNW moisture accelerates wear, so roofs here age faster than in drier climates. We'll tell you honestly which option makes sense for your situation during a free inspection.
